title: galeopsis tetrahit var. bifida
intro: Galeopsis tetrahit var. bifida, also known as dwarf red bartsia or purple bartsia, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Galeopsis tetrahit var. bifida include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve congestion, and soothe the digestive system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations are commonly used in folk medicine to alleviate respiratory and digestive issues.
